title: Sauerbraten Beef Burrito
description: A twist on traditional German sauerbraten, served in a creamy Mexican burrito. Marinated beef is slow-cooked in a mixture of vinegar, water, and spices, then wrapped in a large flour tortilla with creamy toppings.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together beef broth, brown sugar, mustard, and vinegar.
Add the beef strips to the marinade and mix well.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the beef from the marinade and cook in the preheated oven for 2-3 hours, or until tender.
Meanwhile, warm the tortilla by wrapping it in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the shredded cheese and cook until melted and bubbly.
Add the cooked beef to the skillet and stir to combine with the cheese.
Spoon the beef and cheese mixture onto the warmed tortilla, followed by a spoonful of sour cream and a sprinkle of cilantro.
Roll the burrito tightly and serve immediately.
